text:
**3. Working resolver (single source of truth):**
- `src/profile.rs:96-111` — `rewrite_body_mentions(store: &Store, text: &str) -> String`: scans for `nostr:npub1…`/`nostr:nprofile1…` tokens via `nostr_entities` (line 126-143), decodes bech32 with `decode_entity_pubkey` (line 146-154, uses `PublicKey::parse` / `Nip19Profile::from_bech32`), looks up `store.get_profile(&pubkey)`, and replaces the token with `@<name>` (falls back to `pubkey_short`).
- Companion helpers: `body_mention_pubkeys` (src/profile.rs:115-120, collects pubkeys to warm) and `label_chat_senders` (src/profile.rs:74-89, warms cache then rewrites). [...] **2. Existing whitelisted-operator concept**

- `src/config.rs:91-92` — `whitelisted_pubkeys` from config `whitelistedPubkeys`.
- `src/daemon/server.rs:134-137` — `DaemonState::whitelisted_pubkeys()`.
- `src/injection.rs:38-42` — `is_whitelisted(whitelisted, pubkey)`, already used in `render_terminal_mention` (`src/injection.rs:57-78`) to render whitelisted human senders bare as `<@name>` (via `speaker_label`, line 29-36) with **no host at all** — this check is exactly the reusable special-case that both `chat_row_refs`/`chat_log_row_to_json` and `message_row`/`pubkey_ref` currently lack. [...] **1. Unresolved `nostr:npub…` mentions in `channel read`**
`chat_row_to_json` (`src/daemon/server/chat_read_tail.rs`) now rewrites message bodies through the existing `profile::rewrite_body_mentions` resolver before rendering — the same resolver the hook-injected snapshot already used, just never wired into this path. [...] **3. `<Pablo@?>` — the bug you caught mid-fix**
Whitelisted human operators (`whitelisted_pubkeys` in config) have no session/host, so they fell through to the `?` placeholder. `chat_row_refs` now short-circuits to a bare (empty) host for whitelisted pubkeys, and the CLI renderer (`src/cli/messaging.rs`) now prints `<@Pablo>` instead of `<Pablo@?>` when host is empty — mirroring the bare `<@name>` convention already used for terminal-injected mentions in `injection.rs`.
